architectureSummary.overview: CSInvoice is a .NET 6 desktop-style application for generating professional invoices. It uses Unity as the IoC container, PdfSharp/MigraDoc for PDF generation, and SQL Server for storage. The application supports multiple invoice templates and export formats (PDF, Word, Excel).

questions.questionText: CSInvoice uses Unity as an IoC container. How does it compare to ASP.NET Core's built-in DI?
questions.answer:
Unity is a mature IoC container from Microsoft (older than ASP.NET Core's built-in DI). I used it because CSInvoice was a WPF/console application, not an ASP.NET Core app — the built-in DI container only works out-of-the-box with the generic host.

Unity vs Microsoft.Extensions.DI comparison:

FeatureUnityMS.Extensions.DI
Type registrationcontainer.RegisterType<I, C>()services.AddScoped<I, C>()
LifetimeTransientLifetimeManager, ContainerControlledLifetimeManager (singleton), HierarchicalLifetimeManagerTransient, Scoped, Singleton
Property injection✓ supported (via [Dependency])Not natively (use constructor)
Child containers✓ supportedIServiceScope
// Unity registration (CSInvoice)
var container = new UnityContainer();
container.RegisterType<IInvoiceRepository, SqlInvoiceRepository>(
    new HierarchicalLifetimeManager());  // scoped equivalent
container.RegisterType<IPdfGenerator, MigraDocPdfGenerator>(
    new TransientLifetimeManager());

// Resolution
var generator = container.Resolve<IInvoiceGenerator>();

The key concepts are identical — constructor injection, interface-to-implementation binding, lifetime management. The API names differ.
followUpQuestions: Can you use ASP.NET Core DI outside of web applications?
How do you migrate from Unity to Microsoft.Extensions.DI?
questions.importantForInterview: The interviewer wants to know you understand the underlying DI concepts, not just one framework's API. Generic host (Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting) brings MS DI to console/worker apps — so Unity is rarely needed in new .NET projects.

questions.questionText: How did you implement PDF generation in CSInvoice?
questions.answer:
CSInvoice uses MigraDoc (part of the PdfSharp family) for programmatic PDF generation. MigraDoc works at the document object model level — you build a Document object with sections, paragraphs, tables, and MigraDoc renders it to PDF.

public class MigraDocPdfGenerator : IPdfGenerator
{
    public byte[] Generate(InvoiceDto invoice)
    {
        var document = new Document();
        var section = document.AddSection();

        // Header
        var header = section.AddParagraph();
        header.AddText($"INVOICE #{invoice.InvoiceNumber}");
        header.Format.Font.Size = 24;
        header.Format.Font.Bold = true;

        // Invoice details table
        var table = section.AddTable();
        table.AddColumn("6cm");  // Item
        table.AddColumn("3cm");  // Qty
        table.AddColumn("4cm");  // Unit Price
        table.AddColumn("4cm");  // Total

        foreach (var item in invoice.LineItems)
        {
            var row = table.AddRow();
            row.Cells[0].AddParagraph(item.Description);
            row.Cells[1].AddParagraph(item.Quantity.ToString());
            row.Cells[2].AddParagraph(item.UnitPrice.ToString("C"));
            row.Cells[3].AddParagraph(item.Total.ToString("C"));
        }

        // Render to PDF
        var renderer = new PdfDocumentRenderer(unicode: true);
        renderer.Document = document;
        renderer.RenderDocument();

        using var stream = new MemoryStream();
        renderer.PdfDocument.Save(stream);
        return stream.ToArray();
    }
}

I abstracted the PDF generation behind IPdfGenerator so it could be swapped (we evaluated QuestPDF as an alternative later).
followUpQuestions: How does this compare to QuestPDF or iTextSharp?
How do you test PDF generation?
questions.importantForInterview: QuestPDF is the modern alternative — fluent API, better performance, free for non-commercial. iTextSharp requires a commercial license for most use cases. Mention you'd evaluate QuestPDF for a new project.
